title: Boat Rental in Insurance Spain: 2026 Costs, Seasons, and Tips
html:
Navigating the waters off Insurance Spain offers a fresh perspective on the Costa Blanca, with departures from ports such as Altea. BoatHire24 connects you with verified charter operators for a half-day trip starting around €250. Whether you want to explore hidden coves or island-hop, you can book a boat online.

Where to set off in Insurance Spain

Insurance Spain is not a single port but a stretch of coastline near Altea, Calpe, and Moraira. The Marina Port d'Altea is the main departure point for boat hire, with direct access to the Serra Gelada Natural Park. Calpe Marina offers moorings close to the iconic Peñón de Ifach. Moraira's Club Náutico is quieter and ideal for families. Further south, the marina at Villajoyosa provides a base for exploring the coast. The offshore island of Isla de Benidorm is a popular 10‑nautical‑mile run from Altea.

Which boat to rent

  1. RIB speedboats, Best for small groups (up to 6 guests), agile and fuel‑efficient; from €200 for a half‑day.
  2. Motor yachts (Fairline, Azimut), Ideal for couples or corporate trips (up to 8 guests), with cabin amenities; from €650 for a full‑day charter.
  3. Catamarans (Lagoon), Spacious for two families (up to 12 guests), stable and comfortable; from €800 for a full‑day tour.
  4. Sailing yachts, Perfect for seasoned sailors (up to 6 guests), eco‑friendly option; from €350 for a four‑hour trip.
  5. Luxury yachts (Sunseeker), Top‑end experience for up to 10 guests, with crew and water toys; from €1,200 for a full day.

For a reliable choice, compare the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP, a popular 6‑metre open boat with a 150 HP engine, available for hire in Altea.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Insurance Spain?

Prices range from €150 for a shared tour to €1,500 for a crewed luxury yacht per day. BoatHire24 lists clear breakdowns so you can compare options.

  • Shared sunset tour on a small motorboat: from €150 per person, two‑hour trip, max 10 guests.
  • Small motorboat (half‑day, self‑drive): from €250 for up to 6 guests, fuel not included.
  • Catamaran (full‑day, skippered): from €800 for up to 12 guests, includes soft drinks.
  • Crewed motor yacht (full‑day, with skipper): from €1,200 for up to 10 guests, with lunch.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Insurance Spain?

For most small motorboats (under 15 HP or limited power) no licence is required, but the operator will confirm exact requirements.

Local tip: In July and August, the afternoon levante wind can pick up suddenly, so always check the forecast and book a morning slot for calmer conditions.

Skippered charters are available for all boat types, meaning you can relax even without a licence. The high‑season months are June to September, when demand peaks. For self‑drive boats, a valid ID and a refundable deposit are normally required. BoatHire24 provides a secure platform where you can see each operator's licence policy and deposit terms. If you are new to boat hire, the how boat rental works page explains the booking steps clearly.

At a glance

  • Price band: €150 to €1,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day (8 hours)
  • Typical capacity: 6 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (€)
  • Licence required: Depends on boat power; operator confirms

Questions about boat rental in Insurance Spain

Can I moor at Isla de Benidorm?

Yes, but there are no permanent moorings; you anchor off the island and swim or kayak to the shore.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Most operators offer a full refund or reschedule if conditions are unsafe, as per the terms on BoatHire24.

Are there any age restrictions for driving a boat?

Minimum age for self‑drive is usually 21, and a valid driving licence is often required; check with the operator when you book.

Can I rent a boat for a week?

Yes, several operators offer weekly charters for catamarans and motor yachts; prices start from €2,500 for a week in low season.

The coastline of Insurance Spain in 2026 remains a prime destination for boat enthusiasts. To set sail from Altea or Moraira, simply check availability on BoatHire24. If you own a vessel in Insurance Spain, consider listing it on the platform to reach thousands of renters worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Insurance Spain and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
